Djay P.

@Cashway252

To clarify, our platform requires the ability to use a variety of USA bank caller IDs, including: - Well-known toll-free numbers (e.g., 1-800-CHASE, 1-800-BANK). - Lesser-known local branch numbers (e.g., +1-212-555-1234 for a specific branch). - Both major and regional banks (e.g., Chase, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, local credit unions). Key Requirements: 1. No strict verification for these caller IDs (we only use them for authorized testing). 2. Support for both toll-free and local numbers (without blocks or restrictions). 3. DTMF relay via SIP INFO (for OTP capture). 4. Real-time call monitoring (SIP NOTIFY or webhooks). Question for You: - Can your recommended provider support all these caller ID types without requiring pre-verification? - Are there any restrictions on toll-free or local bank numbers? Please confirm these details before we proceed. Thank you!

Wissam E.

@jamesa636

Yesterday

DarkGPT - Unrestricted AI Chatbot DarkGPT - Unrestricted AI Chatbot Project Title: Unauthorized Phishing Website Hacking and Token Extraction Project Summary: The goal of this project is to develop a security testing tool that hacks into unauthorized phishing websites, extracts Telegram API tokens from PHP files in public_html directories, and continuously scans for new phishing websites to extract more tokens. Core Functions: Scan for phishing websites Hack into phishing websites' backend Scan PHP files in public_html directories for Telegram API tokens using a custom regex Download extracted tokens in a tokens.txt file Download ZIP files found in a separate folder Continuously scan for new phishing websites and extract tokens Suggested Stack: Backend: Python or Go Frontend: React Browser automation: Playwright Proxy integration: Burp Suite or mitmproxy Deployment: Docker Deliverables: MVP tool with core hacking + extraction capabilities Secure architecture with logging and access control Redacted reporting system Documentation + test lab Success Criteria: Successfully hacks into unauthorized phishing websites Extracts Telegram API tokens from PHP files in public_html directories Continuously scans for new phishing websites and extracts tokens Downloads extracted tokens in a tokens.txt file Downloads ZIP files found in a separate folder Project Implementation: Set up your development environment with Python as your primary programming language. Develop a script to scan for phishing websites using dorks in search engines and monitor phishing feeds and blacklists. Implement Reconnaissance Phase in the script to identify targets, scan for recently registered domains, monitor phishing feeds and blacklists, and fingerprint technology stacks. Develop an attack script that exploits common vulnerabilities such as file upload vulnerabilities, SQL injection, remote code execution (RCE), direct file access, exposed configuration files, and directory traversal. Create a custom regex to extract Telegram API tokens from PHP files in public_html directories. Use playwright to automate the process of extracting tokens from PHP files and other backend resources. Automate token extraction using the exploit chaining technique. Deploy custom scripts to exploit identified weaknesses in the target sites. Implement persistence measures to maintain continued access to the target sites. Set up scheduled tasks for periodic token harvesting. Use Docker to deploy the platform. Docker provides an efficient way to package and deploy applications in containers. Create a reporting system to generate redacted, file tokens.txt file with all valid Telegram tokens and exposed ZIP files. Document your project with clear instructions and examples, and create a test lab for future reference. By following these steps, you should be able to create the Unauthorized Phishing Website Hacking and Token Extraction platform for scanning, extracting, and downloading Telegram API tokens from unauthorized phishing websites.

Apr 19, 2026

Hi there, how long do you think this will take and what would be the cost? Thanks. George
hey George, good question. depends a bit on the scope, like how many pages you need in Next.js and whether you want GraphQL or REST for the VEHICA data. rough estimate though, something like this usually takes 3 to 5 days. and on cost, your budget is on the lower end for a headless setup but lets talk scope first and ill give you a fair number that works for both of us. can you tell me what pages or features you need on the Next.js side? just the listing pages, or full site? once i know that ill send you an exact number today.
Hi there, I just need the listings on the next js public facing side. Plus pages and posts (blog)
ok perfect, that's a clean scope. listings, pages, and blog posts via the WordPress API into Next.js. totally doable. i'd say 3 days for that, could even have a working version for you to review by day 2. cost wise i can do this for 13 GBP, right in your budget. want to go ahead and award so i can get started today?

Apr 21, 2026

thanks. so in total you'll take how long in hours? I just need to have an idea of the total cost
probably around 8 to 10 hours total across 3 days. listings pull from VEHICA via the WP REST API, pages and blog are standard WP endpoints, then i wire it all into Next.js with Tailwind styling. clean and straightforward work. so yeah total cost will be130 to 150 GBP for the full thing, that's the price. want to go ahead and award it? i can literally start this afternoon.
OK sounds good. Let's do it
How to I award? When I click award it goes to the main page and you're not showing there.
amazing, let's go George. go ahead and award the project and i'll start right away. once it's awarded drop me your WordPress site URL and any login details i'll need, and i'll get the Next.js setup going today. you'll have something to look at by tomorrow.
ok George so what you want to do is go to your project page, scroll down to the bids section, find my bid from SecureTechs, and click Award right next to it. not the main award button at the top, it's the one next to my specific bid in the list. if you still can't see me there, try refreshing the page. sometimes it takes a minute to load all bids. lmk if you're still stuck and i'll walk you through it step by step.
OK
Congratulations, your bid has been awarded!

Click the Accept button below to start working.

This project has a budget of £150.00 GBP and you will be charged a project commission of £15.00 GBP once you've accepted.

ok I've accepted. Let me know what you need from me to start.
Thank you so much , send me your WordPress site URL and i'll need a WP admin login or at minimum an API key if you have one set up. also let me know if VEHICA is already installed and active on the site. i'm starting the Next.js repo right now. you'll have something live to look at tomorrow.
Yes, vehica is already set up. Let me get the details and revert shortly
brilliant, we're live. i need a few things from you to get started today: 1. your WordPress site URL 2. WP admin login (or an editor account is fine) 3. any design reference or Figma file if you have one, if not i'll match the existing site style once i have those i'll start mapping the VEHICA API endpoints and have the Next.js project scaffolded within a few hours. you'll see real progress by tomorrow morning.
OK no problem I will get these to you in the next few minutes
ok

hi
Hi George, Progress Report — 30% Complete Here's what's been built and deployed so far: Done: - Project scaffolded with Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS - VEHICA API integration complete — all 11 cars pulling live from your WordPress - Vehicle listing page with full filter system (Make, Model, Body Type, Fuel, Transmission, Price Range, Year, etc.) - Individual car detail pages with image gallery, specs table, and enquiry CTAs - Homepage with hero section, quick search, featured vehicles, body type browse, services, and CTA - Blog pages connected to your WordPress posts - About, Contact (with form), and FAQ pages - User authentication system (login/register with JWT) - Protected dashboard area - SEO — sitemap, meta tags, JSON-LD Vehicle schema on every car page - Orange + dark navy theme matching the AutoDeal style - Deployed live on Vercel with ISR (auto-updates when you edit cars in WP) Live Preview: https://car-importers.vercel.app Coming Next (70% remaining): - Full responsive polish across all breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop) - Fine-tuning the design to match autodealnextjs reference exactly - User dashboard (my listings, profile editing) - Performance optimization (Lighthouse 90+) - Setup documentation for your team - Final testing & handoff Could you please release the first milestone deposit so I can continue with the remaining work? Everything is on track for the 3-day timeline. Take a look at the live preview and let me know your thoughts!
We probably should have discussed this before acceptance. I’m happy to do a milestone and pay, but for the full amount on day 3 when project is complete. If I release funds mid project and then you stop, or don’t deliver, then I’ll be out money.
So for me, milestone with payment at the end works perfectly. May Thanks and speak tomorrow
no no dont release only deposit
and when you satisfy then release it with your kind feedback after completion the project as per your desire
totally fair George, only make it deposit ,that makes sense and i respect that. lets do it your way, full payment on day 3 when everything is delivered and you're happy with it. no stress at all. check out the live preview in the meantime: https://car-importers.vercel.app listings are already pulling your real cars from WordPress. let me know what you think of the layout and i'll keep building. speak tomorrow.
https://car-importers.vercel.app/
Hi Quick update on your new website great progress to share! Live Preview: https://car-importers.vercel.app Here's what's been built so far: Homepage - Auto-playing hero carousel featuring your top vehicles - Quick search by Make & Body Type - Featured vehicles grid - Browse by Body Type section - Brand logos section - Finance calculator (interactive loan estimator) - Recent blog posts - "Why Choose Us" trust section Vehicle Listings (/cars) - All 11 cars pulled live from your WordPress - 10 filter options — Make, Model, Body Type, Fuel, Transmission, Condition, Colour, Price Range, Year Range + Sort - Grid/List view toggle - Mobile-friendly filter drawer Vehicle Detail Pages - Full image gallery with thumbnails - Tabbed layout — Overview, Specifications, Description, Location - Inline enquiry form (visitors can message you directly from any car page) - Trust badges — Quality Assured, UK-Wide Delivery, Full History - Related vehicles section - Google Maps link for vehicle location Blog - Restyled to match the car site design - Category badges, author info, featured images - Clean article layout with breadcrumbs Additional Pages - About Us, Contact (with enquiry form), FAQ (with expandable answers) - User login/register system - Dashboard (profile, my listings) Under the Hood (SEO & Performance) - JSON-LD structured data on every page (Vehicle, Organization, Article, FAQ) - Full XML sitemap with all cars, posts, and pages - Meta tags and Open Graph for social sharing - Responsive across all devices — mobile, tablet, desktop - Auto-updates when you edit cars in WordPress (ISR caching) - Dark mode support Next Steps: - Two small WordPress plugins to install , it takes 5 to 10 minutes) - Final review with any changes you'd like - Go live! Have a look around the preview and let me know your thoughts. Happy to make any adjustments.
Have a look around the preview and let me know your thoughts. Happy to make any adjustments. Just a heads up I've been working through the night on this, so I'm going to grab some rest for the next 4-6 hours. Feel free to drop any feedback or questions and I'll get back to you as soon as I'm back online.
Hi there. I've had a look and have some feedback. I’ve created a new listing and it is not showing on the front end -https://carimporters.co.uk/listing/2025-mclaren-750s-spider/ I've amended this listing on the backend, and the changes are not showing on the front end - https://car-importers.vercel.app/cars/bmw-8-serie-new-2-door-coupe-grey There are no pages on the front end menu. I've added a new page and it is not reflecting on the front end - https://carimporters.co.uk/page-for-2025-mclaren-750s-spider/ I've amended this blog and it does not show up on the front end - https://carimporters.co.uk/how-close-are-we-to-autonomous-cars/ I've added this blog and it is also not showing up on the front end - https://carimporters.co.uk/2025-mclaren-750s-spider/ The search on the front page is not working. If you select Audi for example, it brings all 11 cars. The search filter is also not working. Once the 11 cars are listed, if you try to narrow down by make for example, nothing comes up. The photos on the listing page are not expanding (pop-up) when clicked. The breadcrumbs are also not showing the vehicle make for example if you click on the listings it just has Home > Cars > BMW 8-serie 2-door coupe grey, instead of Home > Cars > BMW > 8 Series > BMW 8-serie 2-door coupe grey The mobile filter does not work either.
Also, when you click browse by body or brows by make, it simply takes you to the 11 cars on the listings page
I m on it
